channel.title: Notes on Anirudh Vemula
channel.description: Recent content in Notes on Anirudh Vemula

item.title: Online Gradient Descent
item.description: Similar to SGD but different: loss functions are different at each time step Algorithm Projected Online Gradient Descent: $x_1 \in V \subseteq \mathbb{R}^d$ where $V$ is a closed, convex set and $\eta_1, \cdots, \eta_T > 0$ for $t = 1, \cdots, T$ Play $x_t$ Receive $\ell_t: \mathbb{R}^d \rightarrow (-\infty, \infty]$ and pay $\ell_t(x_t)$ Set $g_t = \nabla \ell_t(x_t)$ $x_{t+1} = \Pi_V(x_t - \eta_t g_t) = \arg\min_{y \in V}||x_t - \eta_tg_t - y||_2$ end Regret Analysis Crucial to the analysis is the following simple lemma:

item.title: Frank-Wolfe a.k.a Conditional Gradient Descent
item.description: An alternative approach to projected gradient descent for minimizing a smooth convex function $f$ over a compact, convex set $K$. At the $t$-th step, it considers the first-order taylor approximation $\hat{f}$ of $f$ around $x_t$, and minimizes $\hat{f}$ over $K$, obtaining a minimizer $y_t$. $$ \hat{f}(y) = f(x_t) + \langle \nabla f(x_t), y - x_t \rangle $$ $$ y_t = \arg\min_{y \in K} \hat{f}(y) = \arg\min_{y \in K} \langle \nabla f(x_t), y \rangle $$

item.title: Forward and Reverse KL Divergence
item.description: KL-divergence is given by: $$ D_{KL}(P||Q) = \mathbb{E}_{x \sim P}\left[\log \frac{P(X)}{Q(X)}\right] $$ It is not necessarily symmetric, so $D_{KL}(P||Q) \neq D_{KL}(Q||P)$, thus it cannot be used as a distance metric. It is always positive, i.e. $D_{KL} \geq 0$. Also, if there is a point $x$ where $Q(X) = 0$ but $P(X) \neq 0$, then $D_{KL}(P||Q) = \infty$. Thus, we need the support of $P$ to lie completely in $Q$ for a finite KL-divergence.
